message:
fix(evaluator): tokenize curl detection so 'curlgrep' / 'mycurl' don't trip Railway re-check

Tightens the curl-presence guard introduced in 62ab955. The previous
check was a plain substring contains("curl"), which would falsely
activate the Railway control-plane re-check on any command whose text
happened to contain the four bytes 'c','u','r','l' — including
'curlgrep', 'curlie', 'mycurl', and 'libcurl' references in echo'd
documentation.

command_contains_curl_invocation() now:

- Splits the command on shell-relevant boundaries: ASCII whitespace
  plus `;`, `&`, `|`, `(`, `)`. This catches `cmd1; curl ...`,
  `cmd1 | curl ...`, `(curl ...)`, etc.
- Trims surrounding ASCII single/double quotes from each resulting
  token so that a quoted argv-0 ('curl') still matches.
- Returns true only if any token equals "curl" exactly OR ends with
  "/curl" (so absolute paths like /usr/bin/curl, /opt/homebrew/bin/curl
  match, while /usr/bin/curlie does not).

Adds masked_non_curl_command_name_stays_allowed_for_railway_api_terms
which exercises the exact false-positive case: a curlgrep invocation
with the same Authorization header and Railway GraphQL mutation
payload as the real attack test stays allowed under platform.railway.

Also tweaked the existing masked-documentation test from r#"..."#
to r"..." since its content needs no `#` escaping; pure cosmetic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
